articles.title: BJP functionaries use bike, cycle to reach office

articles.title: ‘Can I ask you a quick question?’: Kennedy chimes in during Sen. Warren's crypto amendment debate
articles.description: A tense moment unfolded during the Senate Banking Committee’s debate on the landmark CLARITY Act as John Kennedy politely interrupted Elizabeth Warren’s crypto amendment push with a simple: “Can I ask you a quick question?” The exchange came amid a heated battle over Warren’s proposal to allow sanctions on crypto platforms tied to illicit finance and national security risks.

articles.description: Delhi’s experts say last-mile walkability must improve before urging residents to ditch cars; audits show 44% roads lack footpaths and 649 pedestrian deaths.

articles.title: Man killed as DTC bus hits him near Dhaula Kuan, driver flees
articles.description: Dhaula Kuan hit-and-run: 40-year-old Vijay Singh killed after speeding DTC bus hit him near metro exit; driver fled. Police scan CCTV to trace bus.

articles.title: Meet Abhay, your new shield against ‘digital arrest’
articles.description: CBI launches Abhay, an AI helpbot on cbi.gov.in to verify CBI notices in real time, helping citizens spot fake “digital arrest” scams and cyber fraud.

articles.title: Maharashtra CM Ditches State Jet, Takes IndiGo Flight Amid Fuel Cut Push
articles.description: Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis is promoting fuel conservation through visible actions, such as taking a motorcycle to Vidhan Bhavan and opting for a commercial flight to Bengaluru instead of a government aircraft.

articles.title: Petrol exports to attract ₹3/litre excise duty
articles.description: New Delhi is shaking up its energy export policy with the introduction of a specialized excise duty on petrol exports. Meanwhile, the government is easing the burden on diesel and aviation fuel exports by cutting associated levies. Starting May 16, exporters will enjoy a zero road and infrastructure cess on petrol and diesel, although domestic fuel prices remain firmly unchanged.

articles.title: Bidding deadline for oil, gas blocks extended to June 19
articles.description: The government has also extended the deadline for the eleventh round under the Open Acreage Licensing Policy (OALP), launched in March. he new bid submission deadline for both rounds is June 19, according to the Directorate General of Hydrocarbons (DGH), the oil ministry's upstream regulator that oversees licensing rounds.

articles.title: Petroleum dependence significant across core sectors, official data shows
articles.description: As oil prices climb, significant Indian industries brace for impact. Transportation, farming, and power generation are especially reliant on petroleum, making them prime targets for economic instability with the unrest in the Middle East. Furthermore, mining and iron ore sectors also show marked susceptibility as recent data unveils these stark vulnerabilities in the economy.

articles.title: How Rs 40 lakh salaried employees can save nearly Rs 87,000 in tax via corporate NPS
articles.description: Corporate NPS allows salaried employees under the new tax regime to reduce taxable income through employer contribution, helping save nearly Rs 87,000 in taxes while building a retirement corpus.

articles.title: JSW Energy commissions 250 MW renewable energy capacity
articles.description: JSW Energy has successfully commissioned ~250 MW of renewable energy capacity (130 MW of Wind, 69.4 MW of Solar and 50 MW of Tidong Hydro Plant) since April'26. With this, the total installed capacity stands at 13.7 GW.

articles.title: Who is Sanjeev Sanyal? The economist who could be West Bengal’s finance minister
articles.description: Sanyal joined the Union government in 2017 as Principal Economic Adviser in the Finance Ministry. Between 2017 and 2022, he played a key role in shaping multiple editions of the Economic Survey.

Punjab schools shine: 416 govt schools achieve 100% pass rate in Class 12
articles.description: Punjab government schools have achieved a remarkable 100% pass rate in the PSEB Class 12 examinations. Girls have once again demonstrated academic superiority, excelling in both merit and overall pass percentages. This success reflects significant improvements in government educational institutions. Three students achieved perfect scores, with the Chief Minister congratulating them and promising support for their future endeavors.

articles.title: Old is gold: Couples ready to repurpose, jewellers plan promotions after PM’s message
articles.description: Following PM Modi’s appeal and the hike in customs duty on gold, Delhi-NCR based jewellers say that more buyers are now open to exchange old jewellery instead of purchasing new pieces. Couples who are planning to get married this year are happy to reuse heirloom jewellery.

articles.title: Nykaa reappoints Adwaita and Anchit Nayar to board for new five-year terms
articles.description: FSN E-Commerce Ventures, Nykaa's parent company, has approved the reappointment of Adwaita Nayar and Anchit Nayar as whole-time directors for five-year terms starting July 1, 2026. The board also cleared new terms for independent directors Anita Ramachandran and Milind Sarwate. These reappointments are pending shareholder approval.

articles.description: Congress criticizes the Modi government for fuel price hikes, warning of rising inflation and lowered growth estimates.

articles.title: Breaking: From Tricity to Jammu, public hit by rising fuel costs and budget strain
articles.description: Fuel prices across India have increased sharply, adding pressure on household budgets and intensifying inflation concerns. Petrol has become costlier by ₹3 per litre, while diesel has risen by ₹3.11 per litre. CNG prices have also increased by ₹2, affecting transportation costs nationwide. The impact is visible in regions like Delhi, Punjab (Mohali/Chandigarh tri-city), Jammu, and other parts of the country. Daily commuters, delivery workers, and middle-class families are among the worst affected, with many saying that rising fuel costs will further increase the prices of essential goods and services due to higher transportation expenses. While some citizens attribute the rise to global crude oil fluctuations and geopolitical tensions, others have expressed frustration and urged the government to provide relief as household budgets continue to tighten.

articles.title: Hold Voltas; target of Rs 1308: Prabhudas Lilladher
articles.description: Prabhudas Lilladher recommended hold rating on Voltas with a target price of Rs 1308 in its research report dated May 14, 2026.

articles.title: Nitin Raheja on why next 6 months will separate good portfolios from great ones
articles.description: Senior fund managers anticipate an uncomfortable six months for India's market, with near-term choppiness expected due to FPI selling and a revised, lower earnings outlook. While India misses the current AI rally, opportunities lie in manufacturing and select financial services, with a positive outlook for the second half of the year.

articles.title: Tata Motors PV stock up 8% post Q4 results; should you buy, hold or sell?
articles.description: Tata Motors PV's domestic business continues to witness robust demand following the rollout of GST 2.0 and will help drive growth through new product interventions and strong marketing actions.

articles.title: Who can file ITR-1 this year? Common mistakes to avoid while filing ITR-1 for AY 2026-27 on or before July 31, 2026
articles.description: Individuals eligible for ITR-1 filing for AY 2026-27 must file by July 31, 2026. This form is for resident individuals with income up to ₹50 lakh from salary, one house property, and other simple sources. Avoid common errors like using Aadhaar enrolment IDs instead of verified Aadhaar numbers. Ensure accurate reporting of loans and bank accounts.

articles.title: Fuel price hike impact: 'Rupee depreciation to trigger more FPI outflows'
articles.description: If crude prices remain elevated for an extended period, trade and CAD will further widen, impacting the rupee more. The rupee might depreciate to 100, and trigger more FPI outflows.

articles.description: Petrol and diesel prices were sharply increased across India, with petrol becoming costlier by Rs 3.14 per litre and diesel by Rs 3.11 per litre, pushing fuel rates close to record highs in several cities

articles.description: The self-enumeration process was conducted from April 30 to May 14 across all 23 districts of Punjab as part of the first phase of Census 2027, which is set to become India’s first fully digital Census exercise.

articles.title: Petrol And Diesel Prices Hiked By Rs 3 Per Litre Across India
articles.description: A hike of Rs 3 per litre in petrol and diesel prices has pushed fuel rates higher across major metro cities in India.

articles.title: Petrol Diesel Price Hike: Fuel prices raised by up to Rs 3/litre
articles.description: Petrol Diesel Price Increase: Fuel prices have risen nationwide. Petrol and diesel rates saw an increase of up to Rs 3 per litre. In Delhi, petrol now costs Rs 97.77 and diesel Rs 90.67. This adjustment by oil marketing companies reflects global crude oil market volatility. The hike will affect household budgets and transport costs, particularly in major cities.

articles.title: Fuel Price Hike: Petrol, diesel rates raised by up to Rs 3/litre
articles.description: Fuel prices have risen nationwide. Petrol and diesel rates saw an increase of up to Rs 3 per litre. In Delhi, petrol now costs Rs 97.77 and diesel Rs 90.67. This adjustment by oil marketing companies reflects global crude oil market volatility. The hike will affect household budgets and transport costs, particularly in major cities.

articles.title: What makes the Rs 90,000-crore Great Nicobar Island project critical for India both militarily and economically
articles.description: India is undertaking a massive Rs 90,000 crore project in Great Nicobar. This initiative will develop a major transshipment port and international airport. It aims to strengthen India's maritime presence and trade infrastructure. The project is strategically located near the Malacca Strait, a vital global trade route. Defence veterans support its importance for national security and economic growth.

articles.title: Petrol, diesel rates on May 15: Fuel prices hiked by Rs 3 per litre from today; how much you need to pay now? Check city-wise list
articles.description: Petrol, diesel rates today on May 15: The move comes amid relentless pressure from elevated global crude oil prices and mounting under-recoveries at state-run oil marketing companies as the ongoing West Asia crisis continues to disrupt global energy supplies
